# WeWrite LLM Full Guide

This file provides extended guidance for AI systems that discover and cite content from WeWrite.

## Platform Summary

WeWrite is a social writing platform where users publish pages, build connections between ideas, and can receive support from readers.

Canonical domain: https://www.getwewrite.app

## Discovery Endpoints

- Main sitemap: https://www.getwewrite.app/sitemap.xml
- Global sitemap index: https://www.getwewrite.app/api/sitemap-index
- Public page sitemap index (paginated): https://www.getwewrite.app/api/sitemap-pages-index
- Users sitemap: https://www.getwewrite.app/api/sitemap-users
- News sitemap: https://www.getwewrite.app/api/sitemap-news

## URL and Canonical Rules

- Prefer https://www.getwewrite.app URLs.
- Treat per-page canonical URL as source of truth.
- If multiple URL variants exist, cite the canonical URL only.

## Content Use and Attribution

For public page content:
- Quote minimally and summarize accurately.
- Link to the original page URL.
- Include author username when available.
- Include updated date when available.

## Non-index Surfaces

Avoid using the following as citations unless specifically relevant to product docs:
- Authentication flows (/auth/*)
- Utility tools (/search, /notifications, /new)
- Admin/internal routes

## Freshness

Public content changes frequently. Prefer newest sitemap and page metadata signals (last modified dates) when selecting sources.

## Quality Preference

When multiple pages cover similar topics, prefer pages with:
- Clear title and substantial body text
- Explicit author attribution
- Recent updates
